tldr: Moving from Akamai to Bunny.net trades enterprise CDN with custom configurations for a simpler, usage-priced edge platform. The cutover is mostly DNS, origin, and cache-rule rebuild plus re-issuing TLS certificates.
why_switch: Lower egress cost for many workloads
Faster onboarding without enterprise sales cycle
what_to_export: WAF rules and bot protection settings
export_method: Pull Property Manager configurations from the Akamai control panel or via the Akamai CLI / EdgeGrid API as JSON. Export each property's behaviors, match criteria, and origin definitions. Inventory hostnames, certificate SAN coverage, and DNS records currently pointing at Akamai. Document WAF rules, custom error pages, redirect logic, and any token-auth secrets — secrets cannot be exported, only re-issued. Save log delivery configurations so you know which downstream destinations to repoint. Treat this as an inventory exercise: the configuration will be rebuilt, not literally imported.
import_method: Create Pull Zones in Bunny.net for each origin, configure hostnames, and provision TLS — Bunny offers managed certificates for most common cases. Translate Akamai cache rules into Bunny Edge Rules: match on path, header, or query, and set TTLs accordingly. Recreate redirects and URL rewrites as Edge Rules. Configure storage zones if you need persistent edge storage. Set up Perma-Cache or Optimizer for image delivery if relevant. Stage the new setup on a test hostname, validate cache hit ratio and origin behavior, then cut DNS over with low TTLs so you can roll back quickly if needed.
effort: 1-4 weeks depending on number of properties, WAF complexity, and TLS coverage
gotchas: WAF rule semantics differ — do not assume a 1:1 port; test with traffic samples
Use low DNS TTLs before cutover so you can roll back quickly
Token auth / signed URL formats are not interchangeable; clients must be updated
Edge compute scripts have to be rewritten in Bunny's runtime
Cache key design affects hit ratio; review before flipping production traffic

email.subject: Cancellation and offboarding from our Akamai contract
email.body:
Hi Akamai team,

We are moving our CDN and edge workloads to another provider over the next 14 days and want to plan the wind-down cleanly. Could you confirm the steps to export our property configurations, certificate metadata, log delivery settings, and any account data we are entitled to before the contract ends?

We would like to schedule cancellation at the end of the current contract term. Please share the date by which we need to give written notice to avoid auto-renewal, the offboarding checklist your team uses, and any data retention timelines that apply after the account closes.

If there are minimum commits or remaining contractual obligations we should be aware of, please flag them.

Thanks,
Your customer
